title: ISE on AWS / Azure / GCP — Cloud-Hosted PSN Health and Egress Cost

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.6 capacity management — cloud capacity monitoring.
Art.28 (ICT third-party risk) — cloud-hosted ISE risk.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Trigger high CPU on a lab cloud PSN. UC-17.1.60 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: CPU returns to normal. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Cloud-host metrics via Splunk Add-on for AWS, Azure, GCP — `index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch` (CPU, network egress) for the EC2 hosting the PSN, etc. ISE supports cloud-hosted PSN deployments since 3.0 (AWS) and 3.1 (Azure/GCP).
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` + cloud TAs (`Splunk Add-on for AWS` / `Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services` / `Splunk Add-on for GCP`).
spl:
(index=cloud sourcetype IN ("aws:cloudwatch", "mscs:metrics", "google:gcp:metrics") metric_name IN ("CPUUtilization", "NetworkOut") instance_tag="ise")
| stats avg(value) as avg max(value) as max by metric_name, instance_id
| where (metric_name="CPUUtilization" AND avg > 70) OR (metric_name="NetworkOut" AND max > 1e9)
description: Detects cloud-hosted ISE PSN resource saturation (CPU >70%) and unusual egress traffic (>1 GB/period) that may indicate misconfigured RADIUS proxying or data exfiltration.
value: Cloud-hosted ISE PSNs introduce egress-cost risk. A misconfigured remote-site RADIUS pointing all auth traffic at a cloud PSN multiplies the cloud egress bill. Catching the egress anomaly early prevents bill surprise.
implementation: Onboard cloud TAs. Tag ISE EC2/VM instances with `ise=true`. Schedule every 30 min.
visualization: (1) Per-instance CPU/Network timechart. (2) Egress trend over 7d. (3) Top-N by egress.

references.title: Cisco ISE on AWS — Deployment Guide

knownFalsePositives:
(a) **Initial deployment** — bootstrap traffic.
(b) **CRL/OCSP refresh** — periodic increased egress.
mitreAttack: N/A (cloud connectivity health — measures ISE-to-cloud session readiness, no attack mapping)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cloud TAs deployed.
• Instance tagging convention.

Step 1 — Configure cloud TA inputs.
Step 2 — Search.
Step 3 — Validate.
Step 4 — Dashboard with cost projection.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting: instance sizing; egress optimisation via VPC peering vs internet egress.
grandmaExplanation: If ISE is running in the cloud — Amazon, Microsoft, Google — we watch the rented computer's busyness AND how much data it sends out. Sending too much data outbound means a big bill or a misconfigured setup.
